verification.target_vocab_notes: できる appears as potential form in the embedded question clause
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains できる potential form (N4 grammar) and かどうか (whether or not) construction which is also N4 level
verification.difficulty_notes: Complex embedded question structure with かどうか. Uses potential form. Multiple clauses with abstract concept about possibility.
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ grammar (できる potential form and かどうか construction). Too advanced for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 春 appears as time expression with に particle
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: demonstrative これら, は topic marker, time expression に, dictionary form 咲く. All basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Simple present tense with time expression. Basic vocabulary (花, 咲く). Short sentence with clear seasonal context.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with clear target vocabulary and appropriate grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: 春 appears as time expression with には double particle
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: 毎年 time expression, は topic marker, time には, i-adjective 暖かい, になる (become). All N5 level
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-advanced N5. Uses になる transformation pattern. Multiple particles and time expressions. Requires understanding of seasonal weather patterns.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate complexity and seasonal vocabulary

verification.target_vocab_notes: 春 appears as the subject that comes
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: の後 (after), location に, が subject marker, 来る verb. Basic time sequence pattern
verification.difficulty_notes: Simple time sequence statement. Basic seasons vocabulary (冬, 春). Short sentence with universal seasonal concept.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example with clear seasonal sequence and simple grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: する appears at the end as the main verb
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: は topic marker, 何でも (everything), する verb. Basic statement structure
verification.difficulty_notes: Uses compound expression しったかぶり (pretending to know/know-it-all). Requires understanding of Japanese compound words. More advanced vocabulary within N5 grammar.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but しったかぶり is a fairly advanced compound expression. Consider if appropriate for N5 learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: する appears as the main verb with を particle
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: は topic marker, time 来年, compound noun 外国旅行, を object marker, する. Basic future action
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-level N5. Future time expression with compound noun 外国旅行. Common travel context. Moderate complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with する verb and appropriate compound noun usage

verification.target_vocab_notes: する appears in the construction にする (to decide on/choose)
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: にする (decide on), のでしょうか question form. The でしょう form is technically N5 for conjecture
verification.difficulty_notes: Uses でしょうか polite conjecture form. Compound expression ペン書き (pen writing). More formal register with conjecture. Higher complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is technically N5 but でしょうか conjecture form and ペン書き compound may be borderline for beginners

verification.target_vocab_notes: 町 appears as the subject of comparison

verification.difficulty_notes: Simple comparison sentence. Basic vocabulary (村, 大きい). Short and clear comparison pattern. Universal concept.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example with clear comparison structure and target vocabulary

verification.target_vocab_notes: 町 appears as the topic of the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: demonstrative この, は topic marker, も particle (both), negative ません. Basic negative statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Uses idiomatic expression about directions (西も東も分からない = completely lost). Requires cultural/idiomatic understanding. Mid-advanced N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but the idiomatic expression about not knowing directions may be culturally complex for beginners

verification.target_vocab_notes: 町 appears in the possessive construction 私たちの町

verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with appropriate complexity and natural town context

verification.target_vocab_notes: This sentence contains おまちかね which comes from 待つ (to wait), not 町 (town). This is a false positive substring match
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly with the Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: が subject marker, よ particle. Grammar is N5 level
verification.difficulty_notes: Uses honorific/polite form おまちかね. Fairly formal expression. Short but requires understanding of polite register.
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary 町 (town) does NOT appear. This is おまちかね from 待つ (wait), not 町. False positive match
